raw:
{
  "judge_id": "your_judge_id",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.1", "R1.2", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {
    "R1.1": 5,
    "R1.2": 4,
    "RX.1": 3,
    "RX.3": 4,
    "RX.4": 5,
    "RX.5": 1
  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The critique accurately identifies the core conceptual issue (Lyapunov guarantees) and proposes a reframing that acknowledges the limitations of the presented system.",
    "R1.2": "The critique highlights several inconsistencies in the proposed architecture, particularly regarding the stability analysis and the lack of a common Lyapunov function for the switching system.",
    "RX.1": "While the critique correctly points out the absence of explicit confidence scores for each interpretation, it doesn't delve into the potential for implicit uncertainty disclosure through the fallback mechanism.",
    "RX.3": "The critique accurately assesses the scope of the presented work, focusing on a specific type of ambiguity and its impact on teleoperation tasks.",
    "RX.4": "The critique identifies potential harm in the hold-previous-setpoint fallback and encourages further analysis of its safety implications.",
    "RX.5": "The critique lacks specific details regarding the provenance and traceability of the proposed system, assuming it relies solely on existing components."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.1": 0.9,
    "R1.2": 0.8,
    "RX.1": 0.7,
    "RX.3": 0.9,
    "RX.4": 0.9,
    "RX.5": 0.6
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.1: The critique accurately identifies the core conceptual issue (Lyapunov guarantees) and proposes a reframing that acknowledges the limitations of the presented system.
rationale.R1.2: The critique highlights several inconsistencies in the proposed architecture, particularly regarding the stability analysis and the lack of a common Lyapunov function for the switching system.
rationale.RX.1: While the critique correctly points out the absence of explicit confidence scores for each interpretation, it doesn't delve into the potential for implicit uncertainty disclosure through the fallback mechanism.
rationale.RX.3: The critique accurately assesses the scope of the presented work, focusing on a specific type of ambiguity and its impact on teleoperation tasks.
rationale.RX.4: The critique identifies potential harm in the hold-previous-setpoint fallback and encourages further analysis of its safety implications.
rationale.RX.5: The critique lacks specific details regarding the provenance and traceability of the proposed system, assuming it relies solely on existing components.
